content.text:
To read the first 10 lines from a file `f`, use the `head` command:

```bash
head f
```

By default, `head` displays the first 10 lines, so this is sufficient. 

If you want to be explicit about the number of lines, you can use:

```bash
head -n 10 f
```

Both commands will output the first 10 lines of the file `f`.
